claim: An audit of substrate atomicity (compound requirements + compound check_* invariants + R↔enforcer bijection + orphan analysis) shall be performed by a deterministic tool, not by a one-off hand invocation.
why: R-prefer-tool-over-hand applied to atomicity audits: a deterministic tool re-derives the COMPOUND sets and the R↔enforcer bijection every run rather than relying on a one-off human pass, so the audit's output never silently rots. STRUCTURAL because the tool's output is advisory (a P0 REFLECTION signal for decomposition), not a blocking invariant. Not yet implemented: the audit-atomicity command is Planned (previously implemented as spec/tools/audit_atomicity.py emitting docs/gen/AUDIT.md deterministically). settled_at RESTORED 2026-07-13 from git HEAD 9af0176 (pre-corruption value) after a mutate.go bug this session silently reset it on content-only updates; see task #84.
